response.justification: The candidate is missing a critical required qualification: native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is explicitly listed as mandatory. Additionally, there is no demonstrated background in embedded security or networking products—all recent experience (7+ years) has been in web, cloud, and AI infrastructure. While the candidate has strong software engineering fundamentals and appropriate seniority, the security-focused nature of the role and embedded networking specifics represent a significant domain expertise gap that would require substantial ramp-up time beyond what a mid-senior hire should require.
strengths: 16+ years of software development experience at staff level across multiple domains, demonstrating systems thinking, infrastructure knowledge, and ability to learn new technical areas quickly
Documented C/C++ experience at National Instruments and prior embedded/hardware work at Altom Consulting; strong Linux and low-level systems familiarity (audio synthesis, DevOps infrastructure)
Proven English proficiency and communication ability through workshops, talks, and open-source contributions; clearly capable of reading technical documentation and written communication
concerns: Missing required native-level Japanese language proficiency—resume lists only English (fluent), Romanian (fluent), and German (basic), making this a hard blocker for the role

Zero demonstrated security engineering background or experience with required security technologies (encryption, authentication protocols, MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, attack/tamper detection); all roles are general software engineering

Recent career trajectory (last 7 years) focused on web/cloud/AI infrastructure; most recent embedded systems work was 6-10 years ago with no evidence of Linux kernel modification, networking switch products, or embedded device firmware experience
